cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the correct answer. The question is asking what we can learn from the text. The options are A, B, C, or D. Let me go through the passage again to make sure I understand the key points. The passage starts by describing Fox Point, a new building in South Bronx, which has a clean and cool boiler room. It mentions that two-thirds of the residents are formerly homeless, and their rent is government-paid. The other residents are low-income families. The boiler room uses special equipment that reuses heat, reducing carbon emissions and cutting costs. It's operated by Palladia, which gets support from Enterprise Community Partners (ECP). ECP has Green Communities Standards that include energy-saving, water conservation, and eco-friendly materials. These standards add 2% to construction costs but save money in the long run. Mayor Bloomberg wants to create 165,000 affordable units because 80% of the city's greenhouse gases come from buildings, and 40% of those are from housing. The DHPD will require all new projects to follow ECP's green standards. Other cities like Cleveland and Denver have similar measures, but New York's DHPD is the largest developer of affordable housing. Now, the options: A) New York City is seriously polluted. The passage does mention greenhouse gas emissions, but it's not saying the city is "seriously polluted" in a general sense. It's more about emissions from buildings. B) People's daily life causes many carbon emissions in New York City. The text says that almost 80% of the city's greenhouse gas emissions come from buildings, and 40% of those are from housing. So, buildings (which include homes and other structures) are a major source. If housing is part of that, then people's daily life (living in homes, using energy, etc.) does contribute significantly. So B might be correct. C) A great number of people in New York City don't have houses to live in. The passage mentions that two-thirds of Fox Point's residents are formerly homeless, and the city is creating affordable units. However, it doesn't state that a great number are homeless now. The problem is more about affordability and housing for low-income, not necessarily that many lack homes entirely. D) Some other cities have developed more affordable housing than New York City. The text says other cities have similar measures, but New York's DHPD is the largest city developer of affordable housing. So New York is the largest, meaning other cities don't have more.
